ItacAcademyItem.Name: Arcidiacono (Accademia dell`)
ItacAcademyItem.Notes: See M. Cavazza, `Dal "Coro anatomico" agli "Inquieti"`, in Quaderni storici (1981) and, by the same author, the biography of its founder `Marsili, Antonio Felice`, in DBI. Marsili resuscitated an Academy that had been originally created in 1656. Marsili`s initiative was advertised in Giornale dei Letterati, 1687, and 1688. The only publication related to the Academy, not in British Library collections, is: Riflessioni filosofiche dedotte dalle figure de` sali dal dottore Domenico Guglielmini espresse in un discorso recitato nell`Accademia filosofica esperimentale di monsig. arcidiacono Marsigli la sera delli 21. marzo 1688...(Bologna, eredi d`Antonio Pisarri, 1688). The Academy was immediately divided into two different branches: Accademia di storia ecclesiastica and Accademia filosofica sperimentale.
ItacAcademyItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
